reasoning: The question asks if 2025 will be the hottest year on record based on NASA GISTEMP data. The current record is held by 2024. As of mid-May 2025, various climate agencies have differing probabilities for 2025 breaking this record. Berkeley Earth maintains an 18% probability as of May 2025. However, NOAA NCEI's most recent Global Annual Temperature Outlook (published May 13) estimates a very low 3% chance that 2025 will be the warmest year on record. April 2025 globally fell short of breaking the record set by April 2024 (by 0.07°C according to both NOAa and Berkeley Earth). The cooling effect of an impending or ongoing La Niña, combined with the fact that 2025 is starting to record temperatures slightly lower than their 2024 counterparts, strongly pushes the probability down. Given that the Polymarket price of 21% is likely anchored to older data (e.g. Berkeley's January forecast which estimated a 30% chance before dropping to 18%), and NOAA's official May statistical projection is just 3%, I am assigning a low probability, but slightly higher than 3% due to the 18% view of Berkeley Earth and some lingering uncertainty regarding La Niña's cooling strength.
